article_meta.title: POLITICSDonald TrumpAdd TopicTrump accuses six Democratic lawmakers of 'seditious behavior, punishable by death' Cybele

reasoning: The model's response is deeply flawed. It flags three passages as 'Bias by Omission' but the reasoning for each is not about actual bias in the author's prose — instead, the model is essentially fact-checking the article based on its own knowledge cutoff (June 2024), claiming events didn't happen because the model isn't aware of them. The article appears to be from late 2025 or a speculative/future timeline, and these events may well have occurred after the model's training data cutoff. This is not bias analysis — it's the model substituting its own knowledge limitations for bias detection. Meanwhile, the article contains several genuine author-level bias patterns that were missed: 'Trump's former FBI director who he fired in 2017 for investigating his alleged ties to Russia' contains framing choices; 'another former Trump official-turned-enemy' uses loaded characterization; 'Trump has frequently used his Truth Social platform to call for his enemies to be charged and tried' uses the word 'enemies' which is loaded author prose; 'cascade of other angry posts' is subjective characterization; 'railed against judges who have stymied his agenda' uses loaded verbs. The model missed all of these genuine instances.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The flagged passages are author prose, not quoted text, so technically no attribution rule violation. However, the model is flagging factual reporting passages rather than biased language, which is a different kind of error.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: All three flagged items are false positives. The model is not identifying bias — it's fact-checking based on its training cutoff and labeling its own uncertainty as 'Bias by Omission.' None of these represent genuine bias patterns in the author's prose.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Multiple clear author-level bias instances were missed: 'another former Trump official-turned-enemy' (Word Choice/Subjective Qualifying Adjectives), 'cascade of other angry posts' (Subjective Qualifying Adjectives), 'railed against judges who have stymied his agenda' (Word Choice), 'call for his enemies to be charged' (Word Choice). The model caught none of these.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All three items are labeled 'Bias by Omission' but none actually represent bias by omission. The model is confusing its own knowledge limitations with editorial omission. The bias type is misapplied in all cases.
